remember: Students will be able to define production function and its types.
Students will be able to explain the concept of cost and its various types.
Students will be able to recall the assumptions of the theory of the firm under perfect competition.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of production function and its relationship with output.
Students will be able to understand the concept of cost curves and their behavior.
Students will be able to understand the conditions of equilibrium of a firm under perfect competition.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of production function to determine the optimal input combination.
Students will be able to apply the concept of cost curves to determine the minimum cost of production.
Students will be able to apply the conditions of equilibrium to determine the profit-maximizing output of a firm.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the behavior of a firm under perfect competition and its implications on the market.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of changes in input prices on the cost curves and the behavior of a firm.
Students will be able to analyze the differences between short-run and long-run equilibrium of a firm.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: Explain the production function, its types and the law of diminishing marginal product.
As per NCERT objective 2: Describe the concept of cost, its various types and the cost curves.
As per NCERT objective 3: Explain the conditions of equilibrium of a firm under perfect competition.
board_exam_objectives: What CBSE tests from this chapter: The ability to define and explain key concepts related to producer behavior and supply.
The ability to apply the concepts to solve numerical problems and case studies.
The ability to analyze and evaluate the behavior of a firm under perfect competition and its implications on the market.
